Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Defendant Irene Lieberman's Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to No. 12.1. The objections are overruled. The response may otherwise be sufficient.
The motion is granted as to No. 12.3 and 12.6. The responses are inadequate.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 12.4, 12.4, 13.1, 50.3, 50.4, and 50.6. The responses are inadequate as each response must be complete at the time it is made. Amendments and supplemental responses must be made in a manner consistent with the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 12.7, 50.1. While Plaintiff may disagree that only these persons have knowledge or that other information is known, it is not subject to an order to respond further.
The motion is granted as to No. 15.1. The objections are overruled. The response is inadequate.
The motion is granted as to No. 16.1.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
The court finds that the responses to the form interrogatories were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Defendants jointly and severally in the amount of $1,375 to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
